title: phaseolus nelsonii
intro: Phaseolus nelsonii, also known as Nelson's bean or wild bean, is a perennial plant species of the legume family that is native to the southwestern United States. The medicinal properties of Phaseolus nelsonii lie in its high concentration of saponins, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of consuming Phaseolus nelsonii include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce inflammation, and support digestive health. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and has also been traditionally used in the form of poultices and salves for topical application.
